title: Grease on Polyester
stainChemistry: Grease is thick fat (kitchen / cooking grease, drippings). Polyester is OLEOPHILIC — fiber that grabs and holds oil tenaciously through hydrophobic interactions. The oleophilic affinity makes grease on polyester one of the harder oil + fiber combos at home: Dawn alone often plateaus, requires powder preload to wick bulk oil up before chemistry. Heat sets oil into polyester permanently AND yellows the fiber over time. The ordering rule: powder preload → Dawn → cool wash → air-dry until fully clear, then dryer. NO hot water until clear. NO chlorine bleach on polyester (yellows fiber).
whyThisWorks: Cornstarch / baking soda preload pulls bulk grease up via capillary action — works even better on oleophilic polyester than on cotton because surface oil hasn't fully penetrated the fiber yet. Dawn emulsifies remaining grease (Dawn is engineered exactly for this). Cool wash + air-dry until verifiably clear prevents heat from setting residual grease into polyester permanently.
defaultAssumption: Polyester garment / blend / kitchen apron / restaurant uniform. Fresh grease = quick win. Hot-dried = permanent. The oleophilic affinity makes timing matter more on polyester than on natural fibers.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover grease with cornstarch / baking soda. 20–30 min absorb. Brush off softly. Repeat with fresh layer if first saturated.
Generous Dawn + cool water. Work into spot with soft toothbrush 10 min — polyester is oleophilic, requires longer dwell. Cool rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Repeat Dawn If Oily Feel Remains
spottingProtocol.instruction: If the spot still feels oily after first Dawn pass (test with clean fingertip), repeat. Polyester often needs 2 passes.
Cool wash per care label. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer. Residual grease + dryer heat = permanent on polyester.

steps: 2. Generous Dawn + soft toothbrush, 10 min cool.
3. Repeat Dawn pass if oily feel remains.
4. Cool wash + air-dry. Inspect before dryer.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets grease + yellows polyester
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets grease into oleophilic polyester permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear
Polyester is oleophilic — expect 2 Dawn passes
Powder preload cuts work in half

homeSolutions.instruction: Polyester is oleophilic — it grabs and holds oil tenaciously. Dawn alone often plateaus on polyester grease. Powder preload (cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth) wicks bulk oil up via capillary action BEFORE Dawn touches it — that's the unlock that makes polyester grease tractable at home. Two rules: NO hot water (sets grease into polyester permanently) and NO chlorine bleach (yellows polyester).

homeSolutions.agent: Cornstarch or Baking Soda Preload — 20 to 30 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the grease spot with a thick layer of cornstarch, baking soda, or plain baby powder. 20 to 30 minutes — fuller's earth (Amazon, ~$10) works even better if you have it. Soft brush off. If the powder visibly saturated, repeat with fresh layer. This step alone often removes most of the stain.

Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap directly to the spot. Work it in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes — polyester needs longer dwell than cotton because of the oleophilic affinity. Cool rinse. Test the spot with a clean fingertip — if it still feels oily, repeat.

homeSolutions.agent: Cool Wash, AIR-DRY Until Fully Clear
homeSolutions.instruction: Cool wash per care label — NOT hot. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE the dryer. Residual grease + dryer heat = permanent on polyester. Once fully clear in daylight, normal dryer is fine going forward.

steps: 1. Cool water only. NO hot water until fully clear.
2. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min. Brush off.
3. Generous Dawn + soft toothbrush, 10 min cool.
4. Repeat Dawn pass if oily feel remains.
5. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect before dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on residual grease (likely permanent on polyester) or if grease has been there for weeks with multiple wash cycles.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets grease + yellows polyester
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear — heat permanent
Polyester loves oil — expect 2 Dawn passes
Powder preload cuts work in half

neverDo: Never hot water on grease + polyester
Never hot dryer until fully clear

escalation.when: Hot dryer used on residual grease, weeks-old grease through multiple wash cycles, or 3 home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Grease on polyester, age [hours / days / weeks], home-treated with cornstarch preload + Dawn × 2 + cool wash. No hot water / no chlorine bleach used. [Residual oily feel / yellow shadow]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with solvent spotting

contextQuestions: Type of grease — kitchen / cooking pan / engine / motor?
How long ago — fresh / dried in / weeks?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Motor oil = different protocol (see motor-oil-* cards). Kitchen grease (this card) is the most common variant. Hot-dried grease = often permanent on polyester due to oleophilic affinity + heat. Pre-applied chlorine bleach = polyester yellowed permanently.
